source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A coil of wire is situated in a uniform magnetic field of flux density $B$. The coil has diameter 3.6 cm and consists of 350 turns of wire, as illustrated in Fig. 9.1. The variation with time $t$ of $B$ is shown in Fig. 9.2. Calculate, for the time $t=0$ to time $t=0.20 \mathrm{~s}$, the electromotive force (e.m.f.) induced in the coil.
extraction_notes: image entries are PIL object representations, not explicit file paths or URLs

image_dependency: question_text explicitly references Fig. 9.1 and Fig. 9.2 for the coil setup and the time-variation of B
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the needed information is not fully recoverable from text alone
the key quantitative input is a graph of B versus t, so solving depends on reading values or slope from the figure
multi_step: solution requires combining coil geometry (diameter to area), number of turns, and magnetic-flux change over the interval t=0 to 0.20 s
the task involves extracting information from the figure and then applying electromagnetic induction relations to compute e.m.f.
this is more than direct lookup because it needs at least graph interpretation plus formula-based calculation
verifiability: answer_type is short_text with a single numeric result, $0.080 \\mathrm{~V}$
the requested output is a standard physics quantity with units, making rule-based or solver-based checking feasible
registry_passed=true, answer_present=true, and there are no recorded asset issues

decision_reasons: 题干、答案和所需图像资产均存在，且注册通过、无资产问题记录
初步评分显示图像依赖高，但相关图像文件完整可用，核心语义支撑充分
题目可验证性高，答案为单一数值型短文本，适合进入候选池
综合来看资产完整度和可恢复性都较好，无需因图像依赖而降为低优先级
